artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow statement about how the central NTNU guideline frames GAI; does not claim approval of any specific tool.
Approved because the source uses explicit disclosure language for GAI use and identifies what should be disclosed.
Approved as a privacy/information-security caution tied directly to source text about GAI inputs, records, unpublished documents, and personal information.
Approved as teaching guidance only; wording preserves the source's advisory language and avoids making it binding.

Approved as a source-status claim based on discovery: a central reference guideline was found, while other official hits were translations, department material, library summaries, news, lectures, or program pages.

artifacts.title: National Taiwan Normal University (NTNU) Generative AI Guidance Staging Notes
artifacts.summary: Staged NTNU claims rely on the official Traditional Chinese CTLD/OAA reference guideline for generative-AI learning applications. The guidance frames GAI as an assistive tool, advises verification and limited reliance, calls for disclosure of GAI use, cautions against entering unpublished documents or personal information, and recommends assessment redesign/consensus where GAI changes evaluation. Discovery did not find a separate formal binding university-wide AI policy; limited department, library, news, lecture, and program sources were rejected or treated as duplicate/context only.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The staged claims are source-language-preserving candidates only and are not marked human reviewed.
Department-specific and event/news sources were not used to infer university-wide binding policy.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
